englishDefinitions: (ambitransitive) To deliver a message by telephone when etiquette demands the effort and respect conveyed by communication in person.
(intransitive) To make a telephone call to a broadcasting station, especially to participate in a program being aired.
(ambitransitive, idiomatic, figurative) To fulfill a responsibility with a minimum effort rather than the appropriate level of effort.
(transitive) To make an unseemly, timid performance; to be restrained and timid when bold action is called for.
persianTranslation: (دوستاگر) برای رساندن پیام از طریق تلفن زمانی که آداب معاشرت مستلزم تلاش و احترامی است که از طریق ارتباط حضوری منتقل می شود.
(غیر گذرا) برقراری تماس تلفنی با ایستگاه صدا و سیما، به ویژه برای شرکت در برنامه در حال پخش.
(دوستانه، اصطلاحی، مجازی) انجام یک مسئولیت با حداقل تلاش به جای تلاش مناسب.
(گذرا) اجرای نامناسب و ترسو. زمانی که نیاز به اقدام جسورانه است، خوددار و ترسو باشند.
examples: Please give us the courtesy of showing up instead of phoning in.
Please give us the courtesy of delivering it in person instead of phoning it in.
We've got Brian phoning in from Phoenix. Brian, you're on the air.
He thought he could phone in the scheduling process, but it takes sharp negotiations around here.
He thought he could phone it in. [pronoun antecedent: the scheduling process]
We're sick of Alice's attitude. She's been phoning it in ever since Bob left. [pronoun antecedent: notional: either "her job" or undefined]
